prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Severe precipitation, a consequence of the La Niña weather pattern, led to widespread flooding in South Africa during February and March of 2023. These inundations impacted seven provinces, specifically the Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West regions. The deluges resulted in human fatalities and significant destruction to residential properties, commercial establishments, essential infrastructure, roadways, and bridges, while also harming agricultural crops and livestock. A minimum of twelve individuals perished across the affected provinces, with others reported as missing. Furthermore, the King Sabata Dalindyebo Local Municipality, situated in the Eastern Cape Province, documented two additional deaths, and one fatality was recorded in Komani Town (previously known as Queenstown). Numerous individuals were relocated from the Lekwa Local Municipality, and inhabitants residing near the Vaal, Vanderkloof, and Bloemhof dams, located along the Orange River in the Northern Cape Province, received recommendations to evacuate following the managed release of water.
B. The La Nia weather phenomenon caused widespread flooding in South Africa during February-March 2023, affecting seven provinces, including Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West. The floods have resulted in casualties and damage to homes, businesses, basic infrastructure, roads, bridges, and affected crops and livestock. At least twelve people have died across the provinces and others are missing. Additionally, evacuations have been made in several areas of King Sabata Local Municipality in Lekwa Local Municipal
C. The floods in South Africa in February-March 2023 were caused by heavy rainfall as a result of the La Niña weather phenomenon, affecting seven provinces, including Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West. The floods have caused casualties and damage to homes, businesses, basic infrastructure, roads, bridges and affected crops and livestock. At least twelve people have died across the provinces and others are missing. In addition to that, two more fatalities have been reported in King Sabata Dalindyebo Local Municipality (Eastern Cape Province), and one person in Komani Town (formerly Queenstown). Several people have been evacuated in Lekwa Local Municipality and residents in the area of Vaal, Vanderkloof, and Bloemhof dams along the Orange River (Northern Cape Province) were invited to evacuate after controlled water

Answer:

A minimum of twelve individuals perished across the affected provinces, with others reported as missing. Furthermore, the King Sabata Dalindyebo Local Municipality, situated in the Eastern Cape Province, documented two additional deaths, and one fatality was recorded in Komani Town (previously known as Queenstown). Numerous individuals were relocated from the Lekwa Local Municipality, and inhabitants residing near the Vaal, Vanderkloof, and Bloemhof dams, located along the Orange River in the Northern Cape Province, received recommendations to evacuate following the managed release of water.
options.B: The La Nia weather phenomenon caused widespread flooding in South Africa during February-March 2023, affecting seven provinces, including Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West. The floods have resulted in casualties and damage to homes, businesses, basic infrastructure, roads, bridges, and affected crops and livestock. At least twelve people have died across the provinces and others are missing. Additionally, evacuations have been made in several areas of King Sabata Local Municipality in Lekwa Local Municipal
options.C: The floods in South Africa in February-March 2023 were caused by heavy rainfall as a result of the La Niña weather phenomenon, affecting seven provinces, including Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West.
